exercises.name: Squat
exercises.description: Focus on depth, keep chest up

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.description: Use a controlled negative (don't free fall) and then explode on the positive.

exercises.name: Lean-back Lat Pulldown
exercises.description: Initiate the pulldown with a straight up posture. As you pull the bar down, lean back by about 15-30° to get the mid-back more involved. Softly touch the bar to your chest on every rep and, even though you're leaning back, still control the weight!

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.description: 1-2 second pause at the bottom of each rep. Instead of just going up onto your toes, think about rolling your ankle back and forth on the balls of your feet.

exercises.name: Machine Crunch(BONUS)
exercises.description: Round your lower back as you crunch. Maintain a mind-muscle connection with your 6-pack.

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Machine Row
exercises.description: Flare elbows out at roughly 45° and squeeze your shoulder blades together hard at the top of each rep.

exercises.name: Pec Deck
exercises.description: Focus on bringing your elbows together - not your hands

exercises.name: Triceps Pressdown(bar)
exercises.description: Focus on squeezing your triceps to move the weight

exercises.name: Bayesian Curl
exercises.description: If you have a left-right bicep size imbalance, do these 1 arm at a time, starting with the weaker arm. Take the weaker arm to the listed RPE. Then match the reps with the other arm (stop once you've matched the reps, even if the RPE is lower). If you don't have a size imbalance, do these both arms at the same time.

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.description: Brace core, push through floor, hips and shoulders rise together

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.description: Set the machine so that you get the biggest stretch possible at the bottom. Prevent your butt from popping up as you curl.

exercises.name: Back Extension
exercises.description: Squeeze your glutes hard at the top of each rep. Slow controlled reps on the way down, followed by an explosive positive.

exercises.name: OHP
exercises.description: Strict press, no leg drive, full lockout

exercises.name: Single-Arm DB Row
exercises.description: Flare your elbows out ~45°. Smooth, controlled reps.

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.description: Focus on squeezing your lateral delt to move the weight.

exercises.name: Face Pulls
exercises.description: Focus on squeezing your face pulls to move the weight.

exercises.name: EZ Bar Skull Crusher
exercises.description: Optionally pause for 0.5-1 second in the stretched aspect of each rep

exercises.name: Incline Press
exercises.description: 45 degree angle, control the descent

exercises.name: Dual-Handle Lat Pulldown
exercises.description: Lean back by ~15° and drive your elbows down as you squeeze your shoulder blades together. This should feel like a mix of lats and mid-traps.

exercises.name: Hammer Curl
exercises.description: Squeeze the rope hard as you curl the weight up. Smooth, controlled reps.

exercises.name: DB concentration curl(BONUS)
exercises.description: Smooth, controlled reps. Mind-muscle connection with the biceps.

exercises.name: Ab Wheel Rollout
exercises.description: Don't just bend at your hips, use your abs to lower yourself down under control and pull yourself back up. If you don't have the core strength to get all the way extended at the bottom, try to progressively increase the ROM week to week.
